steps.title: Fastest path: graph or solve the equation
steps.content:
Type the equation into Desmos as written:
$$\frac{1}{4} \left(x+5\right)-\frac{1}{3} \left(x+5\right)=-7$$Graph or solve it directly and read the x-value that works. The requested value is $79$.

steps.title: Factor the repeated group
steps.content: Both terms contain $(x+5)$, so combine the coefficients first: $$\left(\frac{1}{4}-\frac{1}{3}\right)(x+5)=-7.$$

steps.title: Solve and check choices
steps.content: Multiply by $-12$ to get $x+5=84$, so $x=79$. $79$ is correct. $-5$ would make the left side $0$, not $-7$. The shared factor method avoids distributing fractions separately.
